channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Why Germany wants a seat at the UN Security Council
item.description: Germany seeks a seat on the UN Security Council to increase its global influence. The move is part of Germany's efforts to strengthen its position on the world stage. Why Germany wants a seat at the UN Security Council DW. com.

item.title: Iran executed 21, arrested 4,000 since West Asia war began: U.N.
item.description: Iran has executed 21 people and arrested 4,000 since the West Asia war began, according to a UN report. Many detainees faced forced disappearances, torture, and cruel treatment. The report said many detainees had been victims of forced disappearances, torture or "cruel, inhuman and degrading treatment", including forced confessions.

item.title: Russia says it will stay in OPEC+, hopes U.A.E. exit does not spell end of group
item.description: Russia will remain in OPEC+ despite the UAE's decision to leave. Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov praised OPEC+ as an important organisation during global market turmoil. Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov praised OPEC+ as ‌an important organisation, especially during the current ​turmoil on global markets.

item.title: Amid Iran War and Tensions with Neighbors, U.A.E. Goes Its Own Way
item.description: The UAE's decision to leave OPEC has significant regional implications. The country is charting its own course amid tensions with Saudi Arabia and the ongoing Iran war. The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC has rocked the region, underscoring how the country, at odds with Saudi Arabia, is increasingly charting its own course.

item.title: South Korea appeals court gives seven-year jail term to former President Yoon in arrest obstruction
item.description: A South Korean appeals court sentenced former President Yoon to seven years in jail for obstructing his arrest. The court found Yoon guilty of mobilising the presidential security service to prevent his arrest.

item.title: Iran's rial currency hits record low as shaky ceasefire with US and Israel still holds
item.description: Iran's rial currency hit a record low. The shaky ceasefire with the US and Israel still holds. Iran's rial currency hits record low as shaky ceasefire with US and Israel still holds The Washington Post.

item.title: South Korean court sentences ex-President Yoon to 7 years in prison
item.description: South Korea's appeals court sentenced ex-President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison. He was convicted of resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting. An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.

item.title: Presidents of Cairo Univ., Leiden Univ. discuss cooperation in research field
item.description: Cairo University and Leiden University presidents discussed research cooperation. The meeting aimed to strengthen academic ties. Presidents of Cairo Univ., Leiden Univ. discuss cooperation in research field Egypt Today.

item.title: European Commission president calls for diplomacy to end Iran war
item.description: European Commission president urged diplomacy to end the Iran war. She called for talks to resolve the conflict. European Commission president calls for diplomacy to end Iran war.

item.title: Internet Restrictions Spur Russians to Openly Question Putin’s Moves
item.description: Russians are openly questioning President Vladimir Putin's internet restrictions. Critics range from beauty influencers to the token political opposition. From beauty influencers to the token political opposition, Russians are openly questioning President Vladimir V.

item.title: Ukraine's New Card In War Strategy: Attacks On Russian Oil Infrastructure
item.description: Ukraine has launched attacks on Russian oil infrastructure, targeting the Tuapse refinery which accounts for 12 per cent of Russia's total fuel export capacity. The move is part of Ukraine's new war strategy.

item.title: Afghan women’s refugee team allowed to play in FIFA tournaments
item.description: Afghan Women United, a refugee football team, has been allowed to represent Afghanistan at international competitions, including the LA Olympics. The team can now participate in FIFA tournaments. Afghan Women United can now represent Afghanistan at international football competitions, including the LA Olympics.

item.title: Palestinian football body appeals FIFA ruling on Israel at top sports court
item.description: The Palestinian football body has appealed a FIFA ruling to the top sports court. Visa issues are also affecting Palestinian delegations to the upcoming FIFA Congress. Meanwhile, visa issues are affecting Palestinian and other delegations to the upcoming FIFA Congress.

item.title: Ukraine targets Druzhba pipeline to sever Russian oil, influence in EU
item.description: Ukraine is targeting the Druzhba pipeline to sever Russian oil exports to the EU. Hungary and Slovakia still buy Russian oil, but Ukraine aims to stop this trade and weaken Russian influence in Brussels.

item.title: Hungary's Magyar meets EU's von der Leyen in high-stakes Brussels visit
item.description: Hungary's incoming Prime Minister Peter Magyar met EU Commission President Ursula von der Leyen in Brussels to discuss €10 billion in EU funding. Talks come as part of a post-Orban EU reset and an August funding deadline.

item.title: A Ukrainian accelerator is racing to get arms makers up to NATO standards and into Western defense
item.description: A Ukrainian accelerator is helping arms makers meet NATO standards to access Western defense deals. The effort aims to integrate Ukrainian defense industries into the Western market, potentially increasing their global reach.

item.title: Is the 'security buffer zone' legal?
item.description: The legality of 'security buffer zones' in contemporary warfare is unclear. International humanitarian law does not provide specific rules around buffer zones, leaving their legitimacy in a grey area. Buffer zones occupy a grey area in international humanitarian law, as rules around them in contemporary warfare are not particularly clear.

item.title: 3-year-old rescued after falling into 18-metre-deep well in Syria
item.description: A 3-year-old child was rescued after falling into an 18-metre-deep well in Syria. The child was successfully retrieved from the well, details of the rescue operation are not provided. 3-year-old rescued after falling into 18-metre-deep well in Syria.

item.title: Ludovika Festival to Combine Security Talks and Family Events in Budapest
item.description: The Ludovika Festival in Budapest will combine security talks with family events. The festival aims to bring together discussions on security with community activities, hosting both types of events in one gathering.

item.title: Paraguay Says It’s Not Abandoning Taiwan. China Has Other Plans.
item.description: Paraguay reaffirmed its diplomatic ties with Taiwan, despite China's efforts to strengthen relations with the South American country. China is actively trying to build relationships with countries that currently recognize Taiwan.
